A workshop, not a factory. For thirty-eight years, Artitect Machinery has built folding machines
            the slow way — by hand, in small numbers, with the patience of an
            architect drafting a single line.

Artitect was founded in 1987 by an architect and a metalsmith who
              wanted a folding machine that behaved like a fine instrument.
              Their first double folder, built in a converted barn, still folds
              panels for a Manchester roofing firm today. A workshop, not a factory.

Artitect Machinery began in 1987 in a small atelier outside Manchester,
            with a single double folder and a stubborn belief that a folding
            machine could be both a working tool and a beautiful object. Quietly built. Plainly excellent.
